new_classes.definition_en: A fermented beverage obtained by adding water to grape marc (the solid residue remaining after pressing grapes), classified under CN heading 2206. Piquette is produced by re-fermenting the grape marc with water and is distinct from wine of fresh grapes (heading 2204) and grape marc spirit (heading 2208).

A fermentation process applied to grape marc (the solid residue of pressed grapes) with the addition of water, producing piquette (Tresterwein). This process is distinct from the grape wine production process (which uses fresh grape juice) and from distillation processes used to produce grape marc spirit.

The solid residue of skins, seeds, and stems remaining after the pressing of fresh grapes, used as the primary raw material in the production of piquette (CN 2206 00 10) and grape marc spirit (CN 2208 20).

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2206 00 10 (Piquette/Tresterwein) are: (1) it is a fermented beverage classified under heading 2206, (2) it is produced from grape marc (Trester) by adding water and fermenting — these are captured by the new classes Piquette, GrapeMarcFermentationProcess, and GrapeMarc, and the restrictions linking them. The legal note refers to additional note 9 to Chapter 22, which defines Piquette more precisely (typically specifying that it is obtained by treating grape marc with water and fermenting the resulting liquid), but the full text of additional note 9 was not supplied. Therefore the coverage is reduced to 0.75 because: (a) any numerical thresholds or specific compositional criteria in additional note 9 (e.g. actual alcoholic strength limits, specific process parameters) cannot be captured without the note text; (b) disjointness from grape marc spirit (GrapeDistillationProcess) and from wine of fresh grapes is expressed via complement restrictions on the process class, which approximates but does not fully formalise the legal boundary. The core product identity (grape-marc-based fermented beverage under 2206) is well represented.
